sections.paragraph:
(“expulsion,” i.q. גֵּרְשֹׁון), pr.n.-

(1) of a son of Moses and Zipporah, Exo 2:22 18:3 . In the former place the etymology of this name is alluded to in such a manner that it appears that the writer took it for גֵּר שֹׁם i.q. גֵּר שָׁם a stranger there [this is of course the true etymology; Moses wrote by inspiration, and he knew very well why he gave this name to his own son] (compare ثُمَّ i.q. שָׁם); hence the LXX., that they might express this etymology more distinctly, have put Γηρσάμ.-(2) of a son of Levi, who is elsewhere called גֵּרְשֹׁון which see.-(3) Jud. 18:30 .-(4) Ezr 8:2.
